title: viola pygmaea
intro: Viola pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Violet or Alpine Violet, is a low-growing, perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Viola pygmaea include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and soothe digestive issues. Viola pygmaea is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in infusions, salves, and creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal uses.
